content: Perinatal mental health disorders occur during pregnancy and/or within 12 months postpartum. Postpartum depression (PPD) affects ~10-15% of mothers, characterized by depressed mood, anhedonia, guilt, fatigue, sleep/appetite disturbances, difficulty bonding with infant. Postpartum anxiety and OCD also common. Postpartum psychosis is rare (~1-2/1000 births) but psychiatric emergency involving delusions, hallucinations, confusion, typically onset within 2 weeks postpartum; strong association with bipolar disorder. Risk factors: history of depression/anxiety, inadequate social support, stressful life events, obstetric complications, infant temperament. Untreated perinatal mental illness affects maternal functioning, infant development, family relationships.
